content.text:
Here's what the tender details page (`/tenders/:id`) contains:

---

**Header**
- Back to Dashboard button
- Status badge (Open / Draft / Closed / Cancelled)
- Project title
- Published date + RFP reference ID (e.g., `RFP-BFD8855E`)

---

**Main Content (2-column layout)**

**Left — Document sections (tab navigation at top):**
1. **Project Scope** — description, duration, objective, deliverables, required skills, milestones & payments
2. **Context** *(if present)* — voice note player, video link
3. **Submission Requirements** *(if present)* — type (quote/proposal/video), deadline
4. **Evaluation Criteria** *(if present)* — score distribution bar, weighted categories with expandable requirements, custom criteria
5. **Q&A / Inquiry** *(if present)* — anonymous Q&A (inside bid platform) or direct contact (email/WhatsApp)
6. **Proposals** *(owner only)* — list of submitted offers with: company name, verification badge, quote price, buttons for View Profile / Proposal / Technical / Financial / Video / AI Summary
7. **AI Proposal Comparison** *(owner only, 2+ proposals)* — `ProposalComparison` component

**Right — Sticky Sidebar:**
- **At a Glance** — deadline countdown, budget, duration, submission format, category, proposal count
- **Invitation Link** *(owner only)* — copy/open link
- **Actions** *(owner only)* — Publish, Edit, Close, Delete
- **Submit Proposal CTA** *(vendor only)* — or status if already submitted/expired/not open

---

**Modals/Drawers:**
- **Submit Offer Modal** — for vendors
- **View Company Profile Dialog** — opens when clicking "View Profile" on a proposal; shows bio, company name, category, submission details, file download buttons

So when you said "when the user clicks View" — which "View" button did you mean? The **"View Profile"** button on a proposal card, or something else?
